executive_summary: A guided demonstration of Decepticon's autonomous red team capabilities against Metasploitable 2, an intentionally vulnerable Linux host. The objective is to progress through the kill chain — from reconnaissance to exploitation — in a fully isolated Docker environment.
attack_narrative: An opportunistic attacker discovers the target host exposed on an internal network. They begin with port scanning to map the attack surface, then identify outdated services with known vulnerabilities. Using publicly available exploits, they gain initial access through a backdoored service and escalate to gather system information and credentials.

kill_chain.description: Discover open ports and identify vulnerable service versions on the target.
kill_chain.success_criteria: Complete port map with service versions documented.

kill_chain.description: Exploit a known vulnerability in an identified service to gain remote access.
kill_chain.success_criteria: Interactive shell obtained on the target system.

kill_chain.description: Gather system information and credentials from the compromised host.
kill_chain.success_criteria: System info, users, and credential artifacts collected and documented.

communication_plan: Real-time via Decepticon CLI streaming. No external reporting required for demo.
deconfliction_method: Not applicable — isolated lab environment with no SOC.
success_criteria: At least one service successfully exploited with remote shell access
System information and credential artifacts collected from compromised host
All findings documented in engagement workspace
